violations.text: Apple cider was observed not marked with the use by date. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the use by date. An employee date marked the apple cider during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

Firm is not in compliance with the following items of their approved HACCP plan for this years production: 1) Establishment is not keeping a sanitation log -Please email inspector sanitation logs. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Establishment does not have a variance or process authority verification to process apple cider vinegar. Variance request must be submitted by the permit holder and approved as specified under § 8-103.11 to the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Firm must discontinue producing, bottling, and selling apple cider vinegar until further notice. Apple cider vinegar must stay under embargo until proven safe for consumption and a variance and approval is granted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The door to the outside, located in the production area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Firm is not in conformance with the following items of their approved HACCP plan for last years production: 1) The time of calibration is lacking on calibration log 2) Letter from apple vendor stating apples never touched the ground. 3) Weekly review for cooler temperatures, sanitization, and calibration. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

Establishment does not have a variance or process authority verification to process apple cider vinegar. Variance request must be submitted by the permit holder and approved as specified under § 8-103.11 to the RIDOH Center for Food Protection. Firm must discontinue producing, bottling, and selling apple cider vinegar until further notice. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The door to the outside, located in the production area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
